---
title: "Exercise 5B: Small Model vs. Frontier Model"
subtitle: "MGMT 675: Generative AI for Finance"
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \PassOptionsToPackage{colorlinks=true,urlcolor=blue,linkcolor=blue}{hyperref}
---

Install a small open-source model locally and compare its output to a frontier model on a finance prompt.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Background.}
As we discussed in the Module 10 slides, companies sometimes run open-source models on their own infrastructure for data-security or cost reasons. \href{https://ollama.com}{Ollama} lets you run these models on your laptop with a single command. In this exercise you will install Google's Gemma 4 2B model via Ollama and compare its answer to a frontier model (Claude).

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Setup.}

Download and install Ollama from \href{https://ollama.com}{ollama.com}. Then open a terminal and run:

\begin{verbatim}
  ollama run gemma4:e2b
\end{verbatim}

This downloads the model (about 1.6 GB) and opens a chat session. Type your prompt directly in the terminal.

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{The prompt.}

Give this prompt to both Gemma 4 2B and Claude:

\begin{quote}
\itshape
A private equity firm is evaluating a manufacturing company with \$50M in revenue, 12\% EBITDA margins, and 8\% annual revenue growth. Comparable transactions have been done at 7--9x EBITDA. What is a reasonable valuation range, and what are the top three risks you would flag before proceeding?
\end{quote}

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Deliverables.}

\begin{itemize}
  \item A screenshot of the Gemma 4 response (\texttt{5B-Gemma.png})
  \item A screenshot of the Claude response (\texttt{5B-Claude.png})
  \item A short write-up (1 page max, \texttt{5B-Comparison.pdf}) comparing the two responses: Which was more detailed? More accurate? Did either miss important points? Would the small model be "good enough" for this task?
\end{itemize}
